selected_items.title: Yucatán limestone aquifer system and ocean infiltration
selected_items.why_interesting: Jack describes the subsurface hydrology of Yucatán as a geo-biological signal: porous limestone, underground rivers, cenotes, and ocean water infiltration from the Caribbean. This connects ancient Maya site selection to hidden water currents and earth-biology interfaces.
selected_items.source_claim: The Ocean is seeping beneath Yucatán through limestone. Yucatán is primarily limestone, highly porous, allowing caves, underground rivers, and interconnected cenotes. Ocean water infiltrates through soil and travels through massive underground systems with flooded caverns and hidden currents from the Caribbean across the peninsula. Many areas remain unexplored. Maya saw cenotes as sacred gateways to the underworld connected to forces of nature.
selected_items.mechanism: Limestone porosity enables ocean-freshwater mixing and underground current systems that connect surface and subsurface hydrology across the peninsula.
selected_items.reader_change: Understand Yucatán's subsurface as a living water system where ocean and earth remain connected; recognize cenotes as markers of deep aquifer networks and potential biological significance of limestone-mediated water chemistry.
selected_items.source_support: The Ocean is already seeping beneath the land of Yucatán, and this phenomenon is growing stronger by the day... Yucatán is primarily formed of limestone, a highly porous type of rock that, over thousands of years, has allowed the creation of caves, underground rivers, and interconnected cenotes... enormous flooded caverns and hidden currents that course through the subsurface practically from the Caribbean all the way to various points across the peninsula.
support_quotes: The Ocean is already seeping beneath the land of Yucatán, and this phenomenon is growing stronger by the day.
Yucatán is primarily formed of limestone, a highly porous type of rock that, over thousands of years, has allowed the creation of caves, underground rivers, and interconnected cenotes.
enormous flooded caverns and hidden currents that course through the subsurface practically from the Caribbean all the way to various points across the peninsula.
selected_items.scientific_translation_plan: Explain cenotes as natural sinkholes and water access points in limestone terrain; clarify how porous limestone allows ocean water to mix with freshwater aquifers; describe the biological relevance of mineral-rich underground water systems to Kruse's thesis on water chemistry and earth magnetism.
selected_items.risk_notes: This is a geo observation without explicit treatment protocol. The value is in understanding earth-water systems as biological context, not as a direct medical intervention.

selected_items.title: EDS and internal water table collapse linked to charge accumulation
selected_items.why_interesting: Jack connects Ehlers-Danlos Syndrome pathophysiology to a specific biophysical mechanism: collapsed internal water table (160 to ~78) causing loss of insulation/grounding, leading to excess positive charge accumulation and loss of internal battery capacitance.
selected_items.source_claim: In EDS, the internal water table has collapsed from 160 to approximately 78, causing the patient to become uninsulated and ungrounded, accumulating excess positive charge while the internal battery lacks capacitance to absorb and ground the charge.
selected_items.mechanism: Collapsed internal water table → loss of insulation and grounding → accumulation of positive charge → insufficient capacitance in internal battery to absorb and ground charge.
selected_items.reader_change: Recognize EDS as a water-structure and charge-handling disorder, not only a collagen disorder; understand that grounding and water-table restoration may address the underlying charge dysregulation.
selected_items.source_support: Internal water table has likely collapsed (160 to ~ 78) = she is uninsulated / ungrounded so would accumulate way more + charge and internal batt lacks the capacitance to absorb and ground the charge.
support_quotes: Internal water table has likely collapsed (160 to ~ 78) = she is uninsulated / ungrounded so would accumulate way more + charge and internal batt lacks the capacitance to absorb and ground the charge.
selected_items.scientific_translation_plan: Explain internal water table as the body's hydration and electrical conductivity baseline; clarify how water loss reduces insulation and grounding capacity; describe the internal battery as mitochondrial charge storage and how loss of capacitance leads to charge dysregulation in connective tissue.
selected_items.risk_notes: This is a mechanistic claim about EDS pathophysiology. The writer should present it as Jack's biophysical model, not as proven biochemistry.

selected_items.title: Deuterium stress-adaptation study confirms low-deuterium principle
selected_items.why_interesting: Forum member cites a recent PubMed study showing that high deuterium concentration promotes stress reaction and compensatory adaptation mechanisms, supporting Kruse's thesis on keeping deuterium low.
selected_items.source_claim: A PubMed study titled 'Consumption of Water Similar to Martian Water in Terms of Deuterium Content by Mammals Leads to the Development of an Adaptation Syndrome and Activation of Protective Mechanisms Against Stress' shows that increased deuterium concentration promotes stress reaction and activation of compensatory mechanisms aimed at adaptation.
selected_items.mechanism: High deuterium → stress reaction → compensatory adaptation mechanisms.
selected_items.reader_change: Recognize that elevated deuterium triggers systemic stress and adaptation burden; understand low-deuterium water as a way to reduce this stress load.
selected_items.source_support: An increase in the concentration of deuterium in the body promotes the development of a stress reaction and the activation of compensatory mechanisms aimed at adaptation.
support_quotes: An increase in the concentration of deuterium in the body promotes the development of a stress reaction and the activation of compensatory mechanisms aimed at adaptation.

source_citations.paper: Consumption of Water Similar to Martian Water in Terms of Deuterium Content by Mammals Leads to the Development of an Adaptation Syndrome and Activation of Protective Mechanisms Against Stress
source_citations.claim: Increased deuterium concentration promotes stress reaction and compensatory adaptation

selected_items.scientific_translation_plan: Explain deuterium as a hydrogen isotope in water; clarify that elevated deuterium triggers metabolic stress; describe adaptation syndrome as the body's compensatory response to isotopic burden.
selected_items.risk_notes: Member found and cited the study; Jack did not author this item. Attribute to member discovery and cite the PubMed paper.

selected_items.title: Toddler eczema protocol: solar callus, DHA, seafood exoskeleton broth, L-histidine
selected_items.why_interesting: Member describes a concrete pediatric eczema protocol combining infrared-A morning sun exposure, high-DHA salmon, seafood exoskeleton broth, and L-histidine supplementation to support filaggrin production.
selected_items.source_claim: For toddler eczema, the protocol includes: IR-A and morning sun around sunrise to build solar callus; high-DHA king salmon collar; seafood exoskeleton broth; L-histidine supplementation to support filaggrin, with multiple studies showing positive impact and no side effects.
selected_items.mechanism: Morning IR-A sun exposure → solar callus formation; DHA and seafood exoskeleton → collagen and structural support; L-histidine → filaggrin synthesis and skin barrier function.
selected_items.reader_change: Consider this multi-modal approach for pediatric eczema: prioritize morning sun exposure for IR-A, increase DHA intake via salmon, add seafood exoskeleton broth for collagen precursors, and trial L-histidine supplementation to support skin barrier protein synthesis.
selected_items.source_support: Recently I read Jack's blog about building solar callus. - IR-A, morning sun: we've been getting toddler into the sun around sunrise - feeding him a lot of king salmon collar recently: he likes it and it's super high in DHA - going to try out seafood exoskeleton broth... supplementing with L-histidine yesterday, hoping to help with the filaggrin. There are multiple studies showing positive impact and no side effects.
support_quotes: IR-A, morning sun: we've been getting toddler into the sun around sunrise
feeding him a lot of king salmon collar recently: he likes it and it's super high in DHA
going to try out seafood exoskeleton broth
supplementing with L-histidine yesterday, hoping to help with the filaggrin. There are multiple studies showing positive impact and no side effects.
selected_items.scientific_translation_plan: Explain solar callus as adaptive skin thickening from IR-A exposure; clarify DHA as an omega-3 fatty acid critical for skin barrier; describe filaggrin as a structural protein essential for skin barrier function; explain L-histidine as an amino acid precursor for histamine and skin barrier proteins.
selected_items.risk_notes: This is a member case note, not a Jack-authored protocol. Attribute as a parent's implementation of Kruse principles. The protocol is in early stages (just started L-histidine); do not present as proven.

selected_items.title: April 2026 Science article on cardiac resistance to cancer and metastasis
selected_items.why_interesting: Member cites a recent Science journal article (April 2026) exploring why the heart does not develop cancer or metastasis, framing it as physics trumping biochemistry and requesting Jack's insight on the observation.
selected_items.source_claim: A Science article from April 2026 explores why the heart doesn't get cancer or metastasis, suggesting physics principles override biochemical mechanisms in cardiac tissue.
selected_items.mechanism: Physics-based resistance to cancer in cardiac tissue (mechanism not detailed in source).
selected_items.reader_change: Recognize that cardiac tissue has intrinsic resistance to malignant transformation; understand this as a physics-based property, not only a biochemical one.
selected_items.source_support: April 2026 Science article exploring why the heart doesn't get cancer or metastasis. Another example of physics trumping biochemistry.
support_quotes: April 2026 Science article exploring why the heart doesn't get cancer or metastasis. Another example of physics trumping biochemistry.

source_citations.claim: Heart tissue has intrinsic resistance to cancer and metastasis

selected_items.scientific_translation_plan: Explain metastasis as the spread of cancer cells to distant tissues; clarify why cardiac tissue is uniquely resistant; describe the physics-based mechanism (likely related to electrical properties, charge, or mechanical forces in heart tissue).
selected_items.risk_notes: Member cited the paper and requested Jack's interpretation. The paper itself is the signal; Jack's response is pending. Include the paper link for the writer to access.
